concise_definition: Horns low post offense : Actions initiated from the Horns set, that are utilized to create scoring opportunities, typically for low post players near the basket or alternatively, for offensive players in general near the low post areas.

sections.heading: What is horns low post offense in basketball?
sections.text: Horns low post offense comprises actions initiated from theHornsset, that are utilized to create scoring opportunities, typically for low post players near the basket or alternatively, for offensive players in general near the low post areas.

sections.heading: How to understand the player roles and diagrams on this page
sections.text:
In relation to any diagrams below, each of the offensive players are generally represented by the standardbasketball positionsin number format, unless otherwise noted.

In essence, 1 is thepoint guard; 2 is theshooting guard; 3 is thesmall forward; 4 is thepower forward; 5 is thecenter.

Furthermore, in terms of a brief legend for the diagrams:

- Black Dot or Small Circle:The black dot or small circle represents the basketball.

- Solid Arrow:The solid arrow indicates movement or a cut on the basketball court.

- Dotted Arrow:The dotted arrow represents a basketball pass.

- Wavy Arrow:The wavy arrow indicates dribbling.

- T-Shaped Symbol:The T-shaped symbol signifies a screen, also known as a pick.

- Double pipe symbol:The double pipe symbol denotes handoff action.

sections.heading: What is an example of Horns low post offense that features Spain pick and roll?
sections.text:
Spain pick and rollaction within Horns low post offense could occur after an initial on-ball screen. Furthermore, it’s feasible to utilize astagger screenandhigh lowoption for additional scoring opportunities.

The example diagrams below showcase this with more clarity.

sections.heading: Part 1: Spain pick and roll
sections.text:
[Image Alt Text: An example of Horns low post offense that demonstrates Spain pick and roll action.]

[Image Alt Text: An example of Horns low post offense that demonstrates Spain pick and roll action.]

To begin, 1 dribbles toward the right side wing via the pick set by 5. As that happens, 3 performs azipper cutinto the lane prior to setting aback screen, which is utilized as 5 rolls to the basket, completing the Spain pick and roll.

From there, 5 could receive the ball from 1, particularly as alob passover the top of the defense, which could potentially result in points via analley-oopat the rim.

Otherwise, 5 fills the right side low post area, 4 lifts up to the leftslot, and 3 occupies the right slot.

sections.heading: Part 2: Stagger high low
sections.text:
[Image Alt Text: An example of Horns low post offense that showcases stagger screen and high low action.]

[Image Alt Text: An example of Horns low post offense that showcases stagger screen and high low action.]

Next, 3 and 4 cut towards the left corner to set a stagger screen. After that, 2 uses that screen and cuts to the high post area to receive the ball from 1.

Following that, 5 could duck into the lane, receive the ball from 2 via high low action, and quickly score around the basket.

sections.heading: What is an example of Horns low post offense that includes a back screen?
sections.text:
Aback screenwithin Horns low post offense could occur as a player at the top utilizes a ball screen and a player in the strong side corner cuts towards the lane just below the free throw line.

It’s also possible to implement a cross screen for an additional scoring opportunity.

The example diagrams below illustrate this in more details.

sections.heading: Part 1: Slice
sections.text:
[Image Alt Text: An example of Horns low post offense that demonstrates a back screen followed by a slice-like cut.]

[Image Alt Text: An example of Horns low post offense that demonstrates a back screen followed by a slice-like cut.]

To start, 1 dribbles toward the right side wing area via the pick set by 4 who also replaces at the top.

What’s more, as that happens, 5 slices through the lane to the right side low post area via a back screen set by 2, who also fills theweak sidewing area.

Afterwards, 5 could receive the ball from 1 and score via a low post move near the rim.

sections.heading: Part 2: Reversal and cross screen
sections.text:
[Image Alt Text: An example of Horns low post offense that showcases ball reversal and a cross screen followed by a post entry pass.]

[Image Alt Text: An example of Horns low post offense that showcases ball reversal and a cross screen followed by a post entry pass.]

Next, 1reverses the ballto the top, received by 4, who also reverses to the left side wing, received by 2.

As that happens, 3 cuts into the lane to set a cross screen. That is utilized by 5 who cuts across to thestrong sidelow post area, looking to receive the ball from 2 via the post entry feed.

Following that, 3 spaces out to the empty right side corner while 5 tries to score points via a low post move near the basket.
